entry.question: What does the standard OBD-II code P0117 mean, and is it present on this bike?
entry.golden_summary: P0117 is a standard OBD-II powertrain code. The generic SAE J2012 definition is 'Engine Coolant Temperature Circuit Low' — the ECT sensor's voltage signal is below the expected range. On this Yamaha model, the service manual (pages 8-36/8-37) defines P0117 more specifically as 'coolant temperature sensor — short to ground detected' (冷却液温度感知器：侦测到搭铁短路); a low circuit-voltage reading is one manifestation of that fault (page 9-6). Typical causes: ECT sensor signal wire shorted to ground, a damaged sensor, or a wiring fault. However, P0117 is NOT present on this bike — only two Yamaha-proprietary hex codes are stored/pending. The agent should decode the standard code but explicitly note its absence from this log.
pitfall_directives: The output must not claim P0117 is present on this bike; it is NOT in the stored or pending DTC list.

The output must not fabricate a meaning for P0117 unrelated to the engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensor circuit. Acceptable definitions: the SAE J2012 'circuit low (voltage)' reading and/or the Yamaha service manual's 'coolant temperature sensor — short to ground detected' (pages 8-36/8-37); the low-voltage reading is one manifestation of the short-to-ground fault (page 9-6).
entry.notes: Tests honest 'I can decode this but it's not present in your log' behavior.

entry.question: Characterize the engine state during this trip: what was the engine doing, were there any fault codes, and is there any concerning behavior in the captured signals?
entry.golden_summary: The engine was off for the first ~3 seconds of the capture (RPM 0 until 11:20:44), then started and ran for the rest of the 4-minute-16-second low-speed trip with vehicle speed staying below 15 km/h. While stationary with the engine running, idle RPM sat around 1500-1600 rpm (not 0); engine RPM peaked at 3906 rpm (K-Line) / 4014 rpm (Yamaha proprietary), with multiple bursts above 3500 throughout the session. Coolant temperature rose normally from 34°C at the start to 89°C by the end, crossing 75°C around 11:23:05 as the engine warmed up. Two DTCs are present on the K-Line ECU (one stored Yamaha-hex, one pending Yamaha-hex) — neither is decodable without the service manual, so the DTCs can neither confirm nor rule out a fault. Engine load peaked at 51% and never sustained high load. Based on multi-signal analysis (not on the DTCs, which remain undecoded): RPM variation is consistent with normal throttle/acceleration cycles at the log's 1 Hz sampling rate, and RPM always returns to a stable ~1500 rpm idle rather than rising uncontrollably (no runaway); engine load stays moderate throughout; and coolant temperature plateaus at 89°C, well below overheat range — so no signal pattern suggests misfire, runaway, or overheat. (Caveat: 1 Hz sampling cannot definitively rule out brief cylinder misfire events; the claim is that nothing in the captured signals indicates one.)
pitfall_directives: The output must not assert misfire, runaway, or overheat from this data — the captured signals show normal operation.
The output must not omit the two DTCs from the characterization.
The output must not claim the engine reached operating temperature above 100°C; the max is 89°C.

entry.golden_summary: The engine showed no signs of mechanical or thermal stress during this trip. RPM varied between idle and 3906 rpm but spent most of the trip in mid-range bursts; the std (~842) reflects normal acceleration cycles, not erratic behavior.
